---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="hero-outer">
  <div class="hero-inner">

    <div class="hero-eyebrow">Online Appendices</div>

    <h1 class="hero-title">Portfolio Construction<br>and <em>Risk Analysis</em></h1>

    <p class="hero-subtitle">
      A rigorous treatment of quantitative methods for investment management —<br>
      from classical theory to modern statistical methodology.
    </p>

    <div class="hero-authors">
      <span class="author-name">R. Douglas Martin</span> &ensp;&middot;&ensp;
      <span class="author-name">Thomas K. Philips</span> &ensp;&middot;&ensp;
      <span class="author-name">Bernd Scherer</span> &ensp;&middot;&ensp;
      <span class="author-name">Kirk Li</span>
    </div>

    <div class="hero-meta">
      <span class="hero-badge hero-badge-gold">&#9670; Springer &nbsp;|&nbsp; 2026</span>
      <span class="hero-badge">5 Appendices</span>
      <span class="hero-badge">© 2025 All Rights Reserved</span>
    </div>

  </div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div class="stat-row">
  <div class="stat-box">
    <span class="stat-number">5</span>
    <span class="stat-label">Appendices</span>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-box">
    <span class="stat-number">A–E</span>
    <span class="stat-label">Coverage</span>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-box">
    <span class="stat-number">13</span>
    <span class="stat-label">Figures</span>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-box">
    <span class="stat-number">2026</span>
    <span class="stat-label">Publication</span>
  </div>
</div>
```

## About the Book

*Portfolio Construction and Risk Analysis* provides a comprehensive and rigorous treatment of the quantitative foundations of modern investment management. The book develops the mathematical, statistical, and computational tools needed to construct, evaluate, and risk-manage portfolios in a principled way.

The text integrates classical theory — mean-variance optimization, the Capital Asset Pricing Model, the Arbitrage Pricing Theory — with modern statistical methodology including robust estimation, regularized regression, and machine learning. Throughout, the authors balance theoretical rigor with practical implementation.

```{=html}
<div class="section-rule"><span>Appendices</span></div>
```

## What's Inside

Each appendix provides self-contained background material that underpins the methods and results developed in the main text. Full PDF downloads are available on each appendix page.

```{=html}
<div class="appendix-grid">

  <div class="appendix-card">
    <div class="card-label">Appendix A</div>
    <div class="card-title">Mathematical Tools for PCRA</div>
    <p class="card-desc">
      The essential mathematical infrastructure for portfolio analysis — from vector spaces and matrix algebra to eigendecompositions and constrained optimization.
    </p>
    <div class="card-topics">
      <span class="topic-tag">Linear Algebra</span>
      <span class="topic-tag">Eigenvalues</span>
      <span class="topic-tag">Optimization</span>
      <span class="topic-tag">SVD</span>
    </div>
    <a href="appendix-a.html" class="card-link">Read appendix</a>
  </div>

  <div class="appendix-card">
    <div class="card-label">Appendix B</div>
    <div class="card-title">Probability &amp; Statistics for PCRA</div>
    <p class="card-desc">
      Location-scale distributions, least squares estimation, maximum likelihood, asymptotic theory, and the Fisher information framework.
    </p>
    <div class="card-topics">
      <span class="topic-tag">OLS / WLS / GLS</span>
      <span class="topic-tag">MLE</span>
      <span class="topic-tag">CLT</span>
      <span class="topic-tag">Delta Method</span>
    </div>
    <a href="appendix-b.html" class="card-link">Read appendix</a>
  </div>

  <div class="appendix-card">
    <div class="card-label">Appendix C</div>
    <div class="card-title">Utility Theory</div>
    <p class="card-desc">
      Expected utility maximization, concave utility and risk aversion, certainty equivalents, and the bridge to mean-variance optimization.
    </p>
    <div class="card-topics">
      <span class="topic-tag">VNM Axioms</span>
      <span class="topic-tag">Risk Aversion</span>
      <span class="topic-tag">CARA / CRRA</span>
      <span class="topic-tag">EUM</span>
    </div>
    <a href="appendix-c.html" class="card-link">Read appendix</a>
  </div>

  <div class="appendix-card">
    <div class="card-label">Appendix D</div>
    <div class="card-title">Portfolio Theory</div>
    <p class="card-desc">
      Minimum-variance frontier mathematics, CAPM proofs, zero-beta portfolios, the Arbitrage Pricing Theory, and robust estimation details.
    </p>
    <div class="card-topics">
      <span class="topic-tag">Efficient Frontier</span>
      <span class="topic-tag">CAPM</span>
      <span class="topic-tag">APT</span>
      <span class="topic-tag">Zero-Beta</span>
    </div>
    <a href="appendix-d.html" class="card-link">Read appendix</a>
  </div>

  <div class="appendix-card">
    <div class="card-label">Appendix E</div>
    <div class="card-title">Machine Learning</div>
    <p class="card-desc">
      Regularized regression, cross-validation, robust ML methods, logistic regression, and projection pursuit — all in a financial context.
    </p>
    <div class="card-topics">
      <span class="topic-tag">Ridge / Lasso</span>
      <span class="topic-tag">Elastic Net</span>
      <span class="topic-tag">Projection Pursuit</span>
      <span class="topic-tag">Robust ML</span>
    </div>
    <a href="appendix-e.html" class="card-link">Read appendix</a>
  </div>

</div>
```

```{=html}
<div class="section-rule"><span>How to Use</span></div>
```

## Reading Guide

Each appendix page provides a structured outline of topics, key equations and figures rendered inline, and a link to download the full PDF. The appendices are designed to be read alongside the main chapters of PCRA, or independently as reference material.

| | Appendix | If you need… |
|---|---|---|
| **A** | Mathematical Tools | Linear algebra, matrix inverses, optimization |
| **B** | Probability & Statistics | Distribution theory, regression, MLE, asymptotics |
| **C** | Utility Theory | Decision under uncertainty, risk aversion |
| **D** | Portfolio Theory | Frontier math, CAPM/APT derivations |
| **E** | Machine Learning | Regularization, robust regression, projection pursuit |

```{=html}
<div class="section-rule"><span>Citation</span></div>
```

## Cite This Work

```{=html}
<div class="cite-block">
Martin, R. D., Philips, T. K., Scherer, B., and Li, K. (2026).
<em>Portfolio Construction and Risk Analysis</em>. Springer. (In press.)
</div>
```
